description: Receipt application detail table for Oracle Accounts Receivable. Each record represents the application, unapplication, on-account activity, chargeback, or reversal of a cash receipt against a customer transaction or payment schedule and provides the accounting trail for receipt application history.

columns.name: RECEIVABLE_APPLICATION_ID
columns.description: System-generated identifier for the related receivable application record.

columns.name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.description: User identifier for the person or process that last updated the record.

columns.name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.description: Date and time when the record was last updated.

columns.name: CREATED_BY
columns.description: User identifier for the person or process that created the record.

columns.name: CREATION_DATE
columns.description: Date and time when the record was created.

columns.name: AMOUNT_APPLIED
columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.name: AMOUNT_APPLIED_FROM
columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.name: TRANS_TO_RECEIPT_RATE
columns.description: Rate used to calculate or value trans to receipt for the transaction.

columns.name: GL_DATE
columns.description: Accounting date used when posting the transaction to General Ledger.

columns.name: CODE_COMBINATION_ID
columns.description: Foreign key referencing the General Ledger account combination associated with the accounting entry.

columns.name: SET_OF_BOOKS_ID
columns.description: Ledger identifier in which accounting entries are recorded.

columns.name: DISPLAY
columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.name: APPLY_DATE
columns.description: Business date associated with apply.

columns.name: APPLICATION_TYPE
columns.description: Type classification used to control application processing.

columns.name: STATUS
columns.description: Current processing status of the transaction within the Oracle application flow.

columns.name: PAYMENT_SCHEDULE_ID
columns.description: Foreign key referencing the receivables payment schedule referenced by the application.

columns.name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.description: Login session identifier that performed the last update.

columns.name: CASH_RECEIPT_ID
columns.description: Foreign key referencing the cash receipt header associated with the application or receipt activity.

columns.name: APPLIED_CUSTOMER_TRX_ID
columns.description: Foreign key referencing the receivables transaction header being settled by the application.

columns.name: APPLIED_CUSTOMER_TRX_LINE_ID
columns.description: Foreign key referencing the receivables transaction line being settled by the application.

columns.name: APPLIED_PAYMENT_SCHEDULE_ID
columns.description: Foreign key referencing the payment schedule being affected by the receipt application.

columns.name: CUSTOMER_TRX_ID
columns.description: Foreign key referencing the receivables transaction header to which the record belongs.

columns.name: LINE_APPLIED
columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.name: TAX_APPLIED
columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.name: FREIGHT_APPLIED
columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.name: UNEARNED_DISCOUNT_TAKEN
columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.name: DAYS_LATE
columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.name: APPLICATION_RULE
columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.name: GL_POSTED_DATE
columns.description: Business date associated with gl posted.

columns.name: COMMENTS
columns.description: Free-text explanation entered to document the business purpose of the record.

columns.name: POSTABLE
columns.description: Business attribute of the receipt application used for Oracle EBS transaction processing, control, or reporting.

columns.name: POSTING_CONTROL_ID
columns.description: System-generated identifier for the related posting control record.

columns.name: CONFIRMED_FLAG
columns.description: Indicates whether confirmed applies to the record.

columns.name: PROGRAM_APPLICATION_ID
columns.description: Oracle application identifier for the concurrent program that last updated the record.

columns.name: PROGRAM_ID
columns.description: Concurrent program identifier for the process that last updated the record.

columns.name: PROGRAM_UPDATE_DATE
columns.description: Date and time when the concurrent program last updated the record.

columns.name: REQUEST_ID
columns.description: Concurrent request identifier for the program run that created or updated the record.

columns.name: USSGL_TRANSACTION_CODE
columns.description: Lookup or classification code identifying ussgl transaction for the record.

columns.name: USSGL_TRANSACTION_CODE_CONTEXT
columns.description: Context value used to interpret the USSGL transaction code for federal reporting.

columns.name: CASH_RECEIPT_HISTORY_ID

columns.name: ORG_ID

columns.name: TAX_CODE
columns.description: Lookup or classification code identifying tax for the record.

business_questions: Show how customer receipts were applied to invoices.
Find unapplied or reversed receipt applications.
Display earned and unearned discount amounts taken on applications.
Show receipt applications posted during a period.
Trace a receipt application to the related customer transaction.
Identify on-account and chargeback application activity.
Show tax, freight, and charge portions applied to receivables.
List applications by customer, receipt, or transaction status.
